contextNotes.text: +[[2]] bonus while benefiting from concealment or cover.
+[[2]] bonus while benefiting from concealment or cover.

oEcmHgII4hNU8UJY.description:
Race: Half-Orc
Replaced Trait(s): Intimidating

Characters with this trait slyly cover their body language and movements by acting in a shadowed or partitioned areas. These characters gain a +2 bonus on Bluff and Sleight of Hand checks while benefiting from concealment or cover.

Half-orcs can take this trait in place of intimidating, also gaining the shadow blending fetchling racial trait.
oEcmHgII4hNU8UJY.name: Behind the Veil (Half-Orc)

oMfDPZDS57LKJpHL.description:
Race: Half-Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Base Statistics

Half-elves born to Snowcasters are outcasts from birth, and must struggle to survive the harsh environs of the north either alone or with an adopted family.

Elven Ancestry Snowcaster elves
Human Ancestry Erutaki, Jadwiga, Ulfen, Varki
Regions Crown of the World, Irrisen, Lands of the Linnorm Kings, Varisia
Languages Elven, Erutaki, Hallit, Skald, Varki

Found throughout the northern countries, snowborn are typically the children of twilight speakers—religious envoys from the normally distrustful Snowcaster clans, who consider it a sacred duty to befriend humans and other non-elves. Snowcaster clans vary in their acceptance of these children, but the average clan’s seclusion from humanity and inability to understand half-elves almost invariably ends up driving the snowborn away. Twilight speakers, however, tend to enjoy a particularly close relationship with their snowborn children, and the two groups often wander together. Snowborn have their elven parents’ snowy white skin and particularly sharp ears. Some Snowcaster half-elves find welcome homes among the Jadwiga and Ulfen communities, as well as the Erutaki tribes. Others wander, looking for a permanent place to belong.
oMfDPZDS57LKJpHL.name: Snowcaster Half-Elves (Snowborn)

contextNotes.text: Once per day, you can roll twice and take the better roll.
Once per day, you can roll twice and take the better roll.

oRiG5ZI8BSrKY7i1.description:
Race: Locathah
Replaced Trait(s): Low-Light Vision

Locathahs who dwell in sunlit coastal waters develop close ties with surface dwellers and become skilled negotiators. Once per day when attempting a Diplomacy or Sense Motive check, a locathah with this racial trait can roll twice and use the better result. This racial trait replaces low-light vision.
oRiG5ZI8BSrKY7i1.name: Coastal Emissary

contextNotes.text: once per day, as a free action, you may ignore DR [[10 + @abilities.cha.mod]]

oVggC0XS4INMEUoC.description:
Race: Duskwalker
Replaced Trait(s): Ward against Corruption

A few duskwalkers take on more of their patrons' aspects than others. A duskwalker blessed by an olethros is an agent of fate beyond compare. Once per day as a free action, upon making a successful attack, a duskwalker with this racial trait can ignore an amount of DR equal to 10 + his Charisma modifier for that attack. This racial trait replaces ward against corruption.
oVggC0XS4INMEUoC.name: Olethros's Agent

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Racial bonus to create forgeries.

oaUnqJhO2pV3yvlO.description:
Race: Half-Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Keen Senses

To obtain and keep a position at court, some half-elves aren't shy about exaggerating or fabricating claims to nobility. Half-elves with this racial trait gain a +1 racial bonus on Bluff checks, Knowledge (nobility) checks, and Linguistics checks to create forgeries. They need to spend only half as much on accessories (such as the jewelry required to accessorize a courtier's or noble's outfit) to appear suitable in a courtly setting. This racial trait replaces keen senses.
oaUnqJhO2pV3yvlO.name: Fraudulent

contextNotes.text: Whenever you successfully use Diplomacy to win over an individual, that creature takes a -2 penalty to resist any of your Charisma-based skill checks for the next 24 hours.

obHDRlbtjOSn3oYE.description:
Race: Kitsune
Replaced Trait(s): Agile

Even among your own kind, your gift for making friends stands out. Whenever you successfully use Diplomacy to win over an individual, that creature takes a -2 penalty to resist any of your Charisma-based skill checks for the next 24 hours. This racial trait replaces agile.
obHDRlbtjOSn3oYE.name: Gregarious

ocowKUaIg7ICeykv.description:
Race: Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Base Statistics

Rather than being tied to the terrain around them, some elves are linked to the night itself. Though not tied to demon worship and evil as drow are, these elves are similarly attuned to the magical concepts of darkness and shadow. These elves have the Arcane Focus, Darkvision, Dreamspeaker, and Silent Hunter alternate racial traits.
ocowKUaIg7ICeykv.name: Dusk Elf

ojx2bxS8iepYliML.description:
Race: Gnome
Replaced Trait(s): Gnome Magic, Obsessive

Gnomes who have survived the Bleaching are immune to effects of the Bleaching thereafter. These gnomes gain two favored classes: druid, and their choice of any other class. They lose all spell-like abilities granted by the gnome magic racial trait except speak with animals, but they can use their speak with animals spell-like ability three times per day. This racial ability replaces obsessive and alters gnome magic.

Note

Remove both Gnome Magic and Obsessive
ojx2bxS8iepYliML.name: Bleachling

contextNotes.text: You are @Condition[blind]ed for 1 round if exposed to bright light, and are @Condition[dazzled] as long as you remain in areas of bright light.
You are @Condition[blind]ed for 1 round if exposed to bright light, and are @Condition[dazzled] as long as you remain in areas of bright light.

oqWnE9gSckkN51Wh.description:
Race: Half-Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Low-Light Vision

Some half-elves born of drow parents exhibit more drow traits than others-particularly many of the physical features of the drow-and have darkvision @Distance[60 ft;dual] and light blindness. This racial trait replaces the low-light vision racial trait.

Note

Creatures with light blindness are @Condition[blind]ed for 1 round if exposed to bright light, such as sunlight or the daylight spell. Such creatures are @Condition[dazzled] as long as they remain in areas of bright light.
oqWnE9gSckkN51Wh.name: Drow-Blooded

owvQyYa6BVwKUxwd.description:
Race: Half-Orc
Replaced Trait(s): Darkvision

Many feyborn have low-light vision. Races that normally have darkvision (such as dwarves and half-orcs) can take low-light vision in place of darkvision. In the case of humans, this trait replaces the skilled trait (and the human also gains fey magic and fey thoughts, as detailed in Fey Magic, above).
owvQyYa6BVwKUxwd.name: Low-Light Vision (Half-Orc)

p1Za3owe2q6q4mKg.description:
Race: Human
Replaced Trait(s): Base Statistics

These humans are suited to life in the countryside. Replace the skilled racial trait with heart of the fields and the bonus feat racial trait with focused study.
p1Za3owe2q6q4mKg.name: Country Folk

p5c7fjk9qndNW2Yb.description:
Race: Half-Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Adaptability, Keen Senses

Some drow-blooded half-elves share the drow resistance to magic. A half-elf with this racial trait gains spell resistance equal to 6 + her character level. This racial trait replaces adaptability and keen senses.
p5c7fjk9qndNW2Yb.name: Spell Resistance (Half-Elf)

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] bonus while benefiting from concealment or cover.
+[[2]] bonus while benefiting from concealment or cover.

p61CAuVRzQhhTb2r.description:
Race: Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Low-Light Vision

Characters with this trait slyly cover their body language and movements by acting in a shadowed or partitioned areas. These characters gain a +2 bonus on Bluff and Sleight of Hand checks while benefiting from concealment or cover.

Elves can take this trait in place of low-light vision.
p61CAuVRzQhhTb2r.name: Behind the Veil (Elf)

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Racial bonus whenever you benefit from concealment or full concealment due to darkness or dim light.
+[[2]] Racial bonus whenever you benefit from concealment or full concealment due to darkness or dim light.
+[[2]] Racial bonus whenever you benefit from concealment or full concealment due to darkness or dim light.

p95hpIAifEyv3asz.description:
Race: Halfling
Replaced Trait(s): Weapon Familiarity

Characters with this trait are at home in gloomy conditions. Whenever these characters benefit from concealment or full concealment due to darkness or dim light, they gain a +2 racial bonus on Intimidate, Perception, and Stealth checks.

Halflings can take this trait in place of weapon familiarity.
p95hpIAifEyv3asz.name: Dimdweller (Halfling)

pH0k5yxjVndAoFB6.description:
Race: Dwarf
Replaced Trait(s): Defensive Training, Hatred

Dwarves with this racial trait gain 1 ki point. If the dwarf gains ki points from a different source, this ki point is added to that pool. In addition to any other ways in which the dwarf can use ki, the dwarf can expend the ki point as a swift action to either gain a +2 dodge bonus to AC for 1 round or increase her base speed by @Distance[20 ft;dual] for 1 round. The bonus ki point does not allow the dwarf to make a ki strike unless she has another ability that allows her to do so, such as the ki pool from the monk class. Like other ki points, this ki point is replenished each morning after 8 hours of rest or meditation.

This replaces defensive training and hatred.

Related Buffs:

@Toggle[Iron WIthin (AC)]

@Toggle[Iron WIthin (Speed)]

Note

If you have a ki pool from another source, you could add 1 to that ki pool and link the resource.
pH0k5yxjVndAoFB6.name: Iron Within

pInGb6iceLlnaIu7.description:
Race: Hobgoblin
Replaced Trait(s): Sneaky

Incessant drills make defense second nature to some hobgoblins. Hobgoblins with this racial trait gain a +1 bonus to CMD. This racial trait replaces sneaky.
pInGb6iceLlnaIu7.name: Battle-Hardened

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Racial bonus to escape grapples
+[[2]] Racial bonus vs. grapples

pLmJmahVsCKQT0Jv.description:
Race: Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Elven Immunities

Elves often appear unnaturally thin by other races' standards. Elves with this trait gain a +2 racial bonus on Escape Artist checks, on combat maneuver checks to escape a grapple, and to CMD against grapples. This racial trait replaces elven immunities.
pLmJmahVsCKQT0Jv.name: Slender

pTRlM5BESZuydrGY.description:
Race: Vine Leshy
Replaced Trait(s): Plantspeech; Change Shape

Vine leshys made from poison ivy vines carry natural poison in their bodies. As a swift action, the leshy can imbue its vines with this irritating substance. The next time the leshy hits a creature with an unarmed strike, the creature is affected by the following poison.

Vine Leshy Toxin: Unarmed strike-injury; save Fort DC 10 + half the vine leshy's Hit Dice + the leshy's Constitution modifier; frequency 1/round for 6 rounds; effect sickened for 1 round; cure 1 save.

The leshy can use this ability a number of times per day equal to its Constitution modifier (minimum 1). A vine leshy is immune to its own poison, but not to those of other vine leshys.

This replaces plantspeech and change shape.
pTRlM5BESZuydrGY.name: Poisonous

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] bonus while benefiting from concealment or cover.
+[[2]] bonus while benefiting from concealment or cover.

pYC7FKoEJIuinY8t.description:
Race: Dwarf
Replaced Trait(s): Stonecunning

Characters with this trait slyly cover their body language and movements by acting in a shadowed or partitioned areas. These characters gain a +2 bonus on Bluff and Sleight of Hand checks while benefiting from concealment or cover.

Dwarves can take this trait in place of stonecunning.
pYC7FKoEJIuinY8t.name: Behind the Veil (Dwarf)

pYLyqjaqURyGSyTO.description:
Race: Half-Elf (Spireborn only)

Replaced Trait(s): Languages

All Spireborn begin play speaking Azlanti and Elven. These replace the default half-elf languages.

Note

remove Languages (Half-Elf)
pYLyqjaqURyGSyTO.name: Languages (Spireborn)

pdK0ZRWx2cqBZQGX.description:
Race: Samsaran
Replaced Trait(s): Shards of the Past

You can add spells from another spellcasting class to the spell list of your current spellcasting class. You add a number of spells equal to 1 + your spellcasting class's key ability score bonus (Wisdom for clerics, and so on). The spells must be the same type (arcane or divine) as the spellcasting class you're adding them to. For example, you could add divine power to your druid class spell list, but not to your wizard class spell list because divine power is a divine spell. These spells do not have to be spells you can cast as a 1st-level character. The number of spells granted by this ability is set at 1st level. Changes to your ability score do not change the number of spells gained. This racial trait replaces shards of the past.

Note

You must add the new spells to your class list manually
pdK0ZRWx2cqBZQGX.name: Mystic Past Life

contextNotes.text: -1 penalty on saves against effects that deal positive energy damage.

ph1cjGR44M6Cwycc.description:
Race: Dhampir - Svetocher

Replaced Trait(s): Light Sensitivity (Dhampir)

Alternate Weakness: Svetochers take a -1 penalty on saves against effects that deal positive energy damage.
ph1cjGR44M6Cwycc.name: Alternate Weakness (Dhampir - Svetocher)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Racial bonus vs. non-elven humanoid enchantments

pslPkIsSzbeUteHG.description:
Race: Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Languages

Some elves are so convinced of elven superiority, or else otherwise so xenophobic, that they have difficulty seeing the point in communicating with "lesser" races. Their racial bonus on saving throws against enchantments increases by 1 (to +3) against such effects from non-elf humanoids, but they begin play speaking only Elven, and if they have high Intelligence scores, they can select bonus languages from only Azlanti, Celestial, Draconic, and Sylvan (or Senzar instead of Sylvan if they hail from Tian Xia).

Note

Remove Languages (Elf)
pslPkIsSzbeUteHG.name: Elven Arrogance

qBIFC8W3VkEuxpls.description:
Race: Catfolk
Replaced Trait(s): Sprinter

Catfolk hunters excel at hunting prey from trees and other high vantage points. Catfolk with this racial trait possess a climb speed of @Distance[20 ft;dual] (along with the +8 racial bonus on Climb checks a climb speed affords). This racial trait replaces sprinter.
qBIFC8W3VkEuxpls.name: Climber

qDLgjwzj4XY8fE7e.description:
Race: Oread
Replaced Trait(s): Languages, Subtype, Type

A few ifrits, oreads, sulis, sylphs, and undines have appearances much closer to those of their human ancestors; in fact, they may not even realize their true race. Such geniekin appear to be human, save perhaps minor features like unusual eye color, and they count as humanoid (human) as well as outsider (native) for all purposes (such as humanoid-affecting spells such as charm person or enlarge person). These geniekin do not automatically gain their associated elemental language (but may select it as a bonus language if their Intelligence is high enough). This ability alters the geniekin's type, subtype, and languages.
qDLgjwzj4XY8fE7e.name: Mostly Human (Oread)

qIIJOMsn4Cg222oy.description:
Race: Hobgoblin
Replaced Trait(s): Sneaky

Some hobgoblins scorn caution and subtlety for swagger and bluster. Hobgoblins with this racial trait gain a +4 racial bonus on Intimidate checks. This racial trait replaces sneaky.
qIIJOMsn4Cg222oy.name: Fearsome

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Racial bonus vs. steal and disarm
+[[2]] Racial bonus to notice any attempt to steal from you using sleight of hand; if you notice, this triggers an aoo if you are not flat-footed or if you have combat reflexes.

qJGhs3rHo8bT01FW.description:
Race: Dwarf
Replaced Trait(s): Stability, Stonecunning

Dwarves with this racial trait gain a +4 racial bonus to their CMD against disarm and steal combat maneuvers and a +2 racial bonus on Perception checks to notice Sleight of Hand attempts. If a dwarf with this trait notices a Sleight of Hand attempt to steal from him and the dwarf is not flat-footed or has Combat Reflexes, the attempt triggers an attack of opportunity from that dwarf. This racial trait replaces stability and stonecunning.
qJGhs3rHo8bT01FW.name: Tightfisted

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] racial to look fully human

qJVDmYLzQmuc0v5j.description:
Race: Vishkanya
Replaced Trait(s): Low-Light Vision

You have normal (humanlike) eyes, and your beauty is more conventional. You gain a +4 bonus on Disguise checks to look fully human. This racial trait replaces low-light vision.
qJVDmYLzQmuc0v5j.name: Subtle Appearance

qPvg0VPGlyyi9LBX.description:
Race: Sylph
Replaced Trait(s): Languages, Subtype, Type

A few ifrits, oreads, sulis, sylphs, and undines have appearances much closer to those of their human ancestors; in fact, they may not even realize their true race. Such geniekin appear to be human, save perhaps minor features like unusual eye color, and they count as humanoid (human) as well as outsider (native) for all purposes (such as humanoid-affecting spells such as charm person or enlarge person). These geniekin do not automatically gain their associated elemental language (but may select it as a bonus language if their Intelligence is high enough). This ability alters the geniekin's type, subtype, and languages.
qPvg0VPGlyyi9LBX.name: Mostly Human (Sylph)

qQU7DJtUNw9DkO14.description:
Race: Gnome
Replaced Trait(s): Obsessive

Some gnomes are more academically inclined than their kin. Gnomes with this racial trait gain a +2 bonus on any single Knowledge skill. This racial trait replaces the obsessive racial trait.

Note

Update the untargeted change in the changes tab of this trait to the chosen knowledge skill.
qQU7DJtUNw9DkO14.name: Academician

qfnol8hHibIdZq96.description:
Race: Hobgoblin
Replaced Trait(s): Sneaky

Hobgoblins who failed to secure a position in the military hold the lowest status in hobgoblin society, and quickly learn the value of currying favor with their betters. Hobgoblins with this racial trait gain proficiency in a single martial weapon and +1 racial bonus on Bluff and Diplomacy checks. This racial trait replaces sneaky.

Note

Update the details tab of this trait to include the martial weapon of your choice
qfnol8hHibIdZq96.name: Unfit

qquaaM62KEX4ulIi.description:
Race: Tiefling
Replaced Trait(s): Spell-Like Ability

Some tieflings take on the more bestial aspects of their fiendish ancestors. These tieflings exhibit either powerful, toothy maws or dangerous claws. The tiefling can choose a bite attack that deals 1d6 points of damage or two claws that each deal 1d4 points of damage. These attacks are primary natural attacks. This racial trait replaces the spell-like ability racial trait.

Note

Add a Bite attack or two Claw attacks to your actor, including setting damage on each, manually.

This can be dragged in from Character building>Monster Abilities>Universal Monster rules>Natural Attacks.
qquaaM62KEX4ulIi.name: Maw or Claw

quudBE0oHTdD4ulL.description:
Race: Human
Replaced Trait(s): Base Statistics

While they lack some of the training of other humans, the natural talents of versatile humans more than make up for this lack. Replace the +2 bonus to any ability score, the skilled racial trait, and the bonus feat racial trait with dual talent.
quudBE0oHTdD4ulL.name: Versatile Human

r0tRSOk1uJLAT7b7.description:
Race: Dwarf
Replaced Trait(s): Hardy

Some dwarves train from a young age to outlast orcs on the battlefield. They gain Toughness as a bonus feat and a +1 racial bonus on Fortitude saves. This racial trait replaces hardy.
r0tRSOk1uJLAT7b7.name: Unstoppable

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Racial bonus to pass as surface elf
+[[4]] Racial bonus to pass as surface elf

r6mgpoYE0rnJjrsv.description:
Race: Aquatic Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Keen Senses

Some aquatic elves appear almost indistinguishable from surface elves. Their coloring, height, and weight all suggest those of a surface elf, and the webbing between their digits is almost transparent. Aquatic elves with this racial trait gain a +4 bonus on Bluff and Disguise checks when attempting to pass themselves off as surface elves. This racial trait replaces keen senses.
r6mgpoYE0rnJjrsv.name: Surface Features

r6uTXEULWdyDTxD4.description:
Race: Merfolk
Replaced Trait(s): Base Statistics

The settlement of Outsea is a bizarre environment: a saltwater oasis for merfolk and sahuagin in the freshwater River Kingdoms. Centuries ago, a sahuagin nation from the Inner Sea mobilized a city-sized saltwater bolus for an overland assault on a merfolk state located near modern-day Korvosa. The merfolk learned of this ambitious plan and sent an elite group of soldiers in saltwater tanks to intercept the ocean-bubble. The two sides clashed in Lake Encarthan and eventually worked their way up into what is now the River Kingdoms. In this battle, many of the wizards maintaining the sahuagin's briny mobile fortress perished, and the two sides ended up stranded hundreds of miles from any ocean. Begrudgingly putting aside their grievances, the merfolk and sahuagin worked together to prevent their mutual extinction, holding back the fresh water with spells and building the settlement of Outsea to serve as a permanent home. Now Outsea is a thriving city, home to all kinds of peoples. Centuries of coexistence have worn down the merfolk and sahuagin's hatred for each other, and now they live and work together in peace. Outsear merfolk can take the Unexpected Ally alternate racial trait.
r6uTXEULWdyDTxD4.name: Outsear Merfolk

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] caster level when casting spells with the darkness, light, or shadow descriptor.

rGwCheRNUx1UPAON.description:
Race: Halfling
Replaced Trait(s): Weapon Familiarity

Some illusionists are experts in manipulating light and darkness. Characters with this trait cast spells with the darkness, light, or shadowUM descriptor at +1 caster level.

Halflings can take this trait in place of weapon familiarity.
rGwCheRNUx1UPAON.name: Shadowplay (Halfling)

rIKb0DtfqJkEdBIz.description:
Race: Fetchling
Replaced Trait(s): Spell-Like Abilities

Some fetchlings can manipulate shadowy energy in order to displace their location instead of transporting between shadows. Upon reaching 9th level, instead of gaining shadow walk as a spell-like ability, these fetchlings gain Displacement as a spell-like ability usable twice per day. For this ability, a fetchling's caster level is equal to his total Hit Dice. This racial trait modifies the spell-like ability racial trait.

Note

Replace only the trait Spell-Like Abilities (Fetchling - Shadow Walk)
rIKb0DtfqJkEdBIz.name: Gloom Shimmer

rPbXaUXmsVgZRSTm.description:
Race: Human
Replaced Trait(s): Skilled

Members of some human cultures train from youth to find and follow the trails of vital game and at the same time hide the evidence of their own passage. These humans gain a +2 racial bonus on Stealth and Survival checks, and Stealth and Survival are always class skills for them. This racial trait replaces skilled.
rPbXaUXmsVgZRSTm.name: Practiced Hunter

rXUWNwXKUvWpMToF.description:
Race: Orc
Replaced Trait(s): Light Sensitivity

Orcs refuse to yield to any foe, including the sun. Some spend hour upon hour glaring at the sun until their ruined eyes acclimatize to bright light. Orcs with this racial trait take a -2 penalty on all ranged attack rolls. This racial trait replaces light sensitivity.
rXUWNwXKUvWpMToF.name: Dayrunner

rYjbUx7ne1eLAuZJ.description:
Race: Goblin
Replaced Trait(s): Skilled

In trackless rain forests and marshes, it can be difficult to find dry ground to build on. Goblin tribes living in such areas have learned to live in the treetops. These goblins gain a +4 racial bonus on Acrobatics and Climb checks. This racial trait replaces skilled.
rYjbUx7ne1eLAuZJ.name: Tree Runner

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] bonus to pass as full-blooded drow, elves, or humans.
+[[2]] bonus to pass as full-blooded drow, elves, or humans.

rgteIX0WVM5v9APV.description:
Race: Half-Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Bonus Languages

In benign communities, half-elves endure assumptions and bigotry based on ignorance and suspicion. In drow society, public knowledge of one's mixed ancestry can mean a cruel death. Half-elves with this trait gain a +2 bonus on Bluff and Disguise checks to pass as fullblooded drow, elves, or humans. This racial trait replaces the ability to choose any language as a bonus language, instead limiting the character to the bonus languages offered to drow.
rgteIX0WVM5v9APV.name: Hidden Half-Breed

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Racial bonus vs. undead
+[[2]] bonus vs. spells and spell-like abilities of undead

rkFSqqFnqfXUWjNv.description:
Race: Dwarf
Replaced Trait(s): Defensive Training, Hatred

Some Pahmet come from long lines of guardians who train to defeat any undead that enter the tombs they protect. Dwarves with this racial trait gain a +2 bonus to AC against undead and a +2 bonus on saving throws against spells and spell-like abilities cast by undead.

This replaces defensive training and hatred.
rkFSqqFnqfXUWjNv.name: Death's End

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] bonus on checks made underground.
+[[1]] bonus on checks made underground.

rmiLxAFaM1Sztn7w.description:
Race: Half-Orc
Replaced Trait(s): Intimidating

Some half-orcs live far from the surface, seeking solace in winding cave complexes. Half-orcs with this racial trait gain a +1 bonus on Knowledge (dungeoneering) and Survival checks made underground. This racial trait replaces the intimidating racial trait.
rmiLxAFaM1Sztn7w.name: Cavewight

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Racial bonus whenever benefitting from concealment or full concealment due to darkness or dim light.
+[[2]] Racial bonus whenever benefitting from concealment or full concealment due to darkness or dim light.
+[[2]] Racial bonus whenever benefitting from concealment or full concealment due to darkness or dim light.

rnutFY0fOADYiPah.description:
Race: Human
Replaced Trait(s): Skilled

Characters with this trait are at home in gloomy conditions. Whenever these characters benefit from concealment or full concealment due to darkness or dim light, they gain a +2 racial bonus on Intimidate, Perception, and Stealth checks.

Humans can take this trait in place of the skilled trait, also gaining darkvision to a range of @Distance[60 ft;dual].
rnutFY0fOADYiPah.name: Dimdweller (Human)

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Racial bonus whenever you benefit from concealment or full concealment due to darkness or dim light.
+[[2]] Racial bonus whenever you benefit from concealment or full concealment due to darkness or dim light.
+[[2]] Racial bonus whenever you benefit from concealment or full concealment due to darkness or dim light.

rqHG1kYNsazeEwDc.description:
Race: Half-Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Adaptability

Characters with this trait are at home in gloomy conditions. Whenever these characters benefit from concealment or full concealment due to darkness or dim light, they gain a +2 racial bonus on Intimidate, Perception, and Stealth checks.

Half-elves can take this trait in place of adaptability.
rqHG1kYNsazeEwDc.name: Dimdweller (Half-Elf)

rxDmPBEQ1mH5TzvI.description:
Race: Drow
Replaced Trait(s): Keen Sight

Seduction and treachery are tools for advancement in drow society, even for the martially inclined. Drow with this racial trait may choose either Bluff or Diplomacy as a class skill, and gain a +2 bonus on such skill checks. This racial trait replaces keen sight.

Note

choose either Bluff or Diplomacy. Add the chosen skill as a class skill on the details tab and set the target of the change on the changes tab of this trait.

Note that the text of this racial trait says it replaces "Keen Sight" this is a likely reference to the Keen Senses (Drow) trait.
rxDmPBEQ1mH5TzvI.name: Ambitious Schemer

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Racial bonus to notice a creature using Stealth, as well as Perception checks to notice a weapon being drawn or a hidden trap (or similar signs of danger, subject to the GM's discretion).

s07V8lcVJX3IkC6l.description:
Race: Halfling
Replaced Trait(s): Keen Senses

Song'o halflings have learned to hone in on any indication of danger. Halflings with this racial trait gain a +4 racial bonus on Perception checks to notice a creature using Stealth, as well as Perception checks to notice a weapon being drawn or a hidden trap (or similar signs of danger, subject to the GM's discretion).

This replaces keen senses.
s07V8lcVJX3IkC6l.name: Danger Detection

contextNotes.text: +[[3]] bonus against effects that cause sanity damage.

s8ViF8uZdu09fKJw.description:
Race: Human
Replaced Trait(s): Bonus Feat

Humans have the ability to assuage their own fears by convincing themselves that what they saw or read is not real. Humans with this trait gain a +3 bonus on saving throws against effects that cause sanity damage (see page 12). This racial trait replaces the bonus feat trait.
s8ViF8uZdu09fKJw.name: Rationalize

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Morale bonus on the next single attack roll, saving throw, skill check, or ability check that you makes in the next round, Anytime you cause a creature to catch fire for the first time. subsequent times the creature catches fire provide no bonus.

+[[1]] Morale bonus on the next single attack roll, saving throw, skill check, or ability check that you makes in the next round, Anytime you cause a creature to catch fire for the first time. subsequent times the creature catches fire provide no bonus.

+[[1]] Morale bonus on the next single attack roll, saving throw, skill check, or ability check that you makes in the next round, Anytime you cause a creature to catch fire for the first time. subsequent times the creature catches fire provide no bonus.

+[[1]] Morale bonus on the next single attack roll, saving throw, skill check, or ability check that you makes in the next round, Anytime you cause a creature to catch fire for the first time. subsequent times the creature catches fire provide no bonus.

s9lJ4v97vOu0TiBP.description:
Race: Ifrit
Replaced Trait(s): Fire Affinity

Ifrits with this racial trait derive sadistic satisfaction from watching others burn. Anytime the ifrit causes a creature to catch fire, he gains a +1 morale bonus on the next single attack roll, saving throw, skill check, or ability check that he makes in the next round. The ifrit only gains this bonus the first time he causes a particular creature to catch fire; subsequent times the creature catches fire provide no bonus. This racial trait replaces fire affinity.
s9lJ4v97vOu0TiBP.name: Fire-Starter

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Racial bonus to appear human (standard action)

sNyXq295yfcoKOrb.description:
Race: Undine
Replaced Trait(s): Energy Resistance

Some undines can change their coloration to match human skin tones. As a standard action, an undine with this racial trait can change her natural blue hue to match any normal human skin tone, and can revert to normal as a free action. This grants a +4 racial bonus on Disguise checks to appear human. This racial trait replaces energy resistance.
sNyXq295yfcoKOrb.name: Flesh Chameleon

sOmwse1zXEEj1Wps.description:
Race: Gnome
Replaced Trait(s): Gnome Magic

Gnomes with this racial trait treat their caster level as 1 higher for the purposes of divination spells they cast. Additionally, if the gnome has a Wisdom or Charisma score of 11 or higher, she also gains the ability to cast charm animal as a spell-like ability three times per day. The caster level for this effect is equal to the gnome's level, and the DC for these spells is equal to 10 + the spell's level + the gnome's Charisma modifier.

This replaces gnome magic.
sOmwse1zXEEj1Wps.name: Bleachling Magic

sSd1qkQRLF6erfTf.description:
Race: Aasimar
Replaced Trait(s): Languages, Native Outsider

Some aasimars' heavenly ancestry is extremely distant. An aasimar with this racial trait counts as an outsider (native) and a humanoid (human) for any effect related to race, including feat prerequisites and spells that affect humanoids. She can pass for human without using the Disguise skill. This racial trait replaces the Celestial language and alters the native subtype.
sSd1qkQRLF6erfTf.name: Scion of Humanity

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Racial bonus if at or below half HP and with no conscious ally within 30 ft
+[[2]] Racial bonus if at or below half HP and with no conscious ally within 30 ft

sXLcCq4nFkeFlG5i.description:
Race: Ratfolk
Replaced Trait(s): Swarming

Ratfolk can fight viciously when cut off from friends and allies. Whenever a ratfolk with this racial trait is reduced to half or fewer of his hit points, and has no conscious ally within 30 ft (9 m), he gains a +2 racial bonus on melee attack rolls and to Armor Class. This racial trait replaces swarming.
sXLcCq4nFkeFlG5i.name: Cornered Fury (Ratfolk)

sZWXyODKXOjF5bul.description:
Race: Kobold
Replaced Trait(s): Armor

Kobold sorcerers with the draconic bloodline or kobold bloodline (Advanced Race Guide 134) treat their Charisma scores as 2 points higher for all sorcerer spells and class abilities. This racial trait replaces the armor trait.

Note

You must update the relevant Spells and class abilities manually.
sZWXyODKXOjF5bul.name: Dragon Affinity

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] to forage for food
+[[4]] racial bonus vs. effects that cause nauseated condition

scKsrtwKWy4eGdZx.description:
Race: Goblin
Replaced Trait(s): Skilled

Raised with little or no proper food, many goblins have learned to survive by eating whatever they happen across and can digest nearly anything without getting sick. Goblins with this trait gain a +4 on Survival checks to forage for food and a +4 racial bonus on saves versus effects that cause the nauseated or sickened conditions. This racial trait replaces skilled.
scKsrtwKWy4eGdZx.name: Eat Anything

si86h6jqxQymaE8d.description:
Race: Kitsune
Replaced Trait(s): Kitsune Magic

You were born with an innate talent for switching between your natural forms. Using your racial change shape ability is a move action instead of a standard action. This racial trait replaces kitsune magic.

Note

Update the action cost for your Change Shape (Kitsune) trait manually
si86h6jqxQymaE8d.name: Fast Shifter

siHk5xCzWJ6Cilke.description:
Race: Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Low-Light Vision

Though uncommon, some groups of elves are born with darkvision, rather than low-light vision. In many cases this is taken as a sign of a drow in the elf's ancestry, and can lead to persecution within the elf's home community. Elves with this racial trait gain darkvision with a range of @Distance[60 ft;dual], but also gain sensitivity to light and are dazzled in areas of bright light or within the radius of a daylight spell. This racial trait replaces low-light vision.
siHk5xCzWJ6Cilke.name: Darkvision (Elf)

soX6akWov1I9BaYr.description:
Race: Gnome
Replaced Trait(s): Obsessive

Gnomes with this racial trait gain a +2 racial bonus on a Perform skill of their choice.

This replaces obsessive.

Note

Choose one type of Perform skill. Add this skill to your actor. Update the untargeted change on the changes tab of this trait to the chosen skill.
soX6akWov1I9BaYr.name: Artistic

srbAWQmRwYHPbC57.description:
Race: Shabti
Replaced Trait(s): Immune to Undeath, Native Subtype

A few shabti are made with such skill they are nearly perfect copies of a mortal. Shabti with this racial trait appear to be human, save perhaps for minor features like unusual skin color. They count as humanoid (human) as well as outsider (native) for all purposes (such as humanoid-affecting spells like charm person).

This racial trait replaces immune to undeath and alters the native subtype.
srbAWQmRwYHPbC57.name: Facsimile

sriC2GtR5VyTXYhR.description:
Race: Dhampir
Replaced Trait(s): Spell-Like Ability

A few fortunate dhampirs were born during the day under the blessings of priests, and their blood has weaker ties to their undead bloodline than others of their kind. Such dhampirs are unhindered by daylight and lose the light sensitivity racial trait. This racial trait replaces the spell-like ability racial trait.

Note

Remove both Spell-Like Ability (Dhampir) and Light Sensitivity (Dhampir)
sriC2GtR5VyTXYhR.name: Dayborn

t1hlo7DrquwiDhd1.description:
Race: Human
Replaced Trait(s): Skilled

Fey-touched humans enjoy senses and reactions superior to those of their kin. These humans receive low-light vision, gain a +1 racial bonus on Reflex and Will saves, and treat Knowledge (nature) and Perception as class skills. This racial trait replaces skilled.
t1hlo7DrquwiDhd1.name: Heart of the Fey

tBAYyT2iIvWot43d.description:
Race: Shabti
Replaced Trait(s): Resist Level Drain

Crafted to suffer punishments intended for their masters, some shabti can use this mortal connection to serve as reciprocal conduits. As an immediate action after being afflicted by temporary negative levels, a shabti with this racial trait can force the creature that inflicted the negative levels to suffer the same cumulative penalties, hit point loss, and caster level reduction. The shabti can use this ability only on an enemy within @Distance[60 ft;dual] and can share penalties with only one creature at a time. The shabti can sever this link as a free action.

This racial trait replaces resist level drain.
tBAYyT2iIvWot43d.name: Soul Sympathy

tC0JCXIRZK9tEoMm.description:
Race: Half-Orc
Replaced Trait(s): Weapon Familiarity

Half-orcs with this trait know little of their orc ancestry and were raised among humans and other half-orcs in a large city. City-raised half-orcs are proficient with whips and longswords, and receive a +2 racial bonus on Knowledge (local) checks. This racial trait replaces weapon familiarity.
tC0JCXIRZK9tEoMm.name: City-Raised

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Racial bonus against curse effects and hexes. This bonus stacks with the bonus granted by halfling luck.

tF9ythkMrLwffISo.description:
Race: Halfling
Replaced Trait(s): Fearless

Halflings with this trait receive a +2 racial bonus on saving throws against curse effects and hexes. This bonus stacks with the bonus granted by halfling luck. This racial trait replaces fearless.
tF9ythkMrLwffISo.name: Blessed

tH5MSvbiO5EBzMbV.description:
Race: Kitsune
Replaced Trait(s): Agile, Languages

Although most kitsune speak only Common, some quickly learn other human languages in order to give themselves an edge when dealing with humanity. They speak Common, Senzar, and the racial language of their human form's ethnicity, and they can learn any language they want (except Druidic and other secret languages) if they have high Intelligence scores. This racial trait replaces agile as well as a kitsune's normal languages.

Note

Add the Human ethnic language on the details tab of this trait manually
tH5MSvbiO5EBzMbV.name: Multilingual

tHxA1j59mDXdYanV.description:
Race: Gathlain
Replaced Trait(s): Spell-Like Abilities

Some gathlains can use their unusual mentality as a defense against efforts to control their minds. Once per day when a gathlain with this trait attempts a Will saving throw, he can roll the saving throw twice and use the better result. He must decide to use this ability before attempting the saving throw.

This replaces the spell-like abilities racial trait.
tHxA1j59mDXdYanV.name: Whimsical Outlook

tOyZJ3QAQqZDdMxX.description:
Race: Halfling
Replaced Trait(s): Base Statistics

Hailing from the bureaucratic Degasi city of Segada, Segadan halflings relish the comforts their prosperous city provides. The halflings of Segada nonetheless remain suspicious of Chelish settlers in the Segadan protectorates of Anchor's End and Canorus, given Cheliax's history of enslaving halflings. Segadan halflings are well suited to take the attentive alternate racial trait, as well as the festive and small quarter ally alternate racial traits described below.
tOyZJ3QAQqZDdMxX.name: Segadan Halflings

tQ76ZcUN6Sk1hYHY.description:
Race: Vine Leshy
Replaced Trait(s): Ability Scores

Agile vine leshys are quicker but less tough than a typical vine leshy. Replace the leshy's +2 racial bonus to Constitution with a +2 racial bonus to Dexterity.

This alters the vine leshy's ability scores.

Note

Remove: Ability Scores (Vine Leshy)
tQ76ZcUN6Sk1hYHY.name: Agile (Vine Leshy)

contextNotes.text: You reduce the penalty for moving by 5, and the penalty for sniping by 10.

tQKF5kI0sfD2qSa6.description:
Race: Halfling
Replaced Trait(s): Sure-Footed

Halflings possess incredible stealth even while moving through obstructed areas. Halflings with this racial trait reduce the penalty for using Stealth while moving by 5, and reduce the Stealth check penalty for sniping by 10. This racial trait replaces the sure-footed racial trait.
tQKF5kI0sfD2qSa6.name: Swift as Shadows

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] bonus when underground.
+[[2]] bonus vs traps and hazards when underground.

tTnTbwQMUCVklGGl.description:
Race: Tiefling
Replaced Trait(s): Spell-Like Ability

Those who brave the lightless tunnels below Golarion's surface learn to identify the dangerous phenomena that characterize the Darklands. Characters with this trait gain a +2 bonus on initiative checks, and on saves against traps and hazards when underground (from a lifetime of dodging accursed pools, cave-ins, and green slime).

Tieflings can take this trait in place of the darkness spell-like ability.
tTnTbwQMUCVklGGl.name: Darklands Guide

contextNotes.text: You do not take any penalties for using improvised weapons.

tf7HYWI0ohY5oSpe.description:
Race: Halfling
Replaced Trait(s): Sure-Footed, Weapon Familiarity

Halflings with this trait do not take any penalties for using improvised weapons. This trait counts as the Catch Off Guard or Throw Anything feats for the purpose of qualifying for feats. This racial talent replaces sure-footed and weapon familiarity.
tf7HYWI0ohY5oSpe.name: Resourceful

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Racial bonus against evil creatures.
+[[2]] Racial bonus against evil creatures.
+[[2]] Racial bonus against evil creatures.

uDlZlUlvcf9EE1ys.description:
Race: Half-Orc
Replaced Trait(s): Intimidating, Orc Ferocity

Some half-orcs know how monsters think. They receive a +2 racial bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, and Sense Motive checks against evil creatures. This racial trait replaces intimidating and orc ferocity.
uDlZlUlvcf9EE1ys.name: Monstrous Sympathy

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] bonus while benefiting from concealment or cover.
+[[2]] bonus while benefiting from concealment or cover.

uPfUcu59YuGhaoFX.description:
Race: Half-Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Low-Light Vision

Characters with this trait slyly cover their body language and movements by acting in a shadowed or partitioned areas. These characters gain a +2 bonus on Bluff and Sleight of Hand checks while benefiting from concealment or cover.

Half-elves can take this trait in place of low-light vision.
uPfUcu59YuGhaoFX.name: Behind the Veil (Half-Elf)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Racial bonus while adjacent to ally with less than 1/2 HP
+[[1]] Racial bonus while adjacent to ally with less than 1/2 HP

uSED8jnh3FejGkiw.description:
Race: Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Elven Magic, Weapon Familiarity

While some Forlorn quickly become inured to the loss and tragedy all too common in their lives, some refuse to let go, fighting passionately when companions are endangered. Elves with this alternate racial trait gain a +1 racial bonus on attack rolls and caster level checks while adjacent to an ally who has been reduced to fewer than half her maximum hit points.

This replaces elven magic and weapon familiarity.
uSED8jnh3FejGkiw.name: Devoted Companion

uXWtABSsuB1zn67j.description:
Race: Orc
Replaced Trait(s): Ferocity, Weapon Familiarity

Orcs with this racial trait gain a limited scent ability with half the normal range. This racial trait replaces ferocity and weapon familiarity.
uXWtABSsuB1zn67j.name: Smeller

ua3zeWweXn3jTe64.description:
Race: Half-Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Keen Senses

Half-elves with this trait practice magical techniques to conceal their fey features from casual scrutiny. These half-elves treat their caster level as 1 higher when casting illusion spells. Half-elves with a Charisma score of 11 or higher also gain the following spell-like abilities 1/day-guidance, innocence, lullaby, and mage hand. The caster level for these effects is equal to the half-elf 's level. The DC for these spells is equal to 10 + the spell's level + the half-elf 's Charisma modifier. This racial trait replaces keen senses.
ua3zeWweXn3jTe64.name: Disregarded

ubKGM0XjI0u10yyK.description:
Race: Kasatha
Replaced Trait(s): Jumper, Stalker

Kasatha living on Golarion must often take great pains to hide their existence from others. Such kasathas gain a +2 racial bonus on Stealth checks. Stealth is always a class skill for them. This racial trait replaces jumper and stalker.
ubKGM0XjI0u10yyK.name: Stealthy

ucIzgwFkg6OqaBif.description:
Race: Hobgoblin
Replaced Trait(s): Sneaky

Hobgoblins raised in the unusually open and stable nation of Kaoling or among the slave-drivers of Isger's Chitterwood often develop a knack for controlling others. They gain a +2 racial bonus on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks. Both skills are always class skills for them. This racial trait replaces sneaky.
ucIzgwFkg6OqaBif.name: Authoritative

contextNotes.text: Can always take 10

ugVzv2fQAGFUy6Si.description:
Race: Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Elven Magic, Keen Senses

Mordant Spire elves learn to dive and swim at an early age. Elves with this trait gain a +2 racial bonus on Swim checks and can always take 10 on a Swim check, even if distracted or endangered when swimming. This racial trait replaces elven magic and keen senses.
ugVzv2fQAGFUy6Si.name: Natural Swimmer

uj0JvFUtrmpOSNVa.description:
Race: Skinwalker
Replaced Trait(s): Base Statistics

Ancestry werecrocodile
Typical Alignment NE
Ability Modifiers +2 Int, -2 Wis (+2 Str while shapechanged)
Alternate Skill Modifiers Stealth, wild empathy
Alternate Spell-Like Ability scare 1/day
Bestial Features

  • Bite attack that deals 1d6 points of damage
  • Darkvision to a range of @Distance[60 ft;dual]
  • Swim speed of @Distance[30 ft;dual]
  • Ferocity (Pathfinder RPG Bestiary 300)
uj0JvFUtrmpOSNVa.name: Werecrocodile-Kin (Scaleheart)

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] caster level when casting spells with the earth descriptor.

urZZ6gwdm34pDxuT.description:
Race: Dwarf
Replaced Trait(s): Stonecunning

Some dwarves' affinity for the earth grants them greater powers. Dwarves with this racial trait are treated as one level higher when casting spells with the earth descriptor or using granted powers of the Earth domain, the bloodline powers of the deep earth bloodline or earth elemental bloodline, and revelations of the oracle's stone mystery. This ability does not give them early access to level-based powers; it only affects the powers they could use without this ability. This racial trait replaces stonecunning.

Note

Add the bonus for each related spell, class ability, or domain power manually.
urZZ6gwdm34pDxuT.name: Stonesinger

uvFRWmR4IU1kPbyM.description:
Race: Changeling
Replaced Trait(s): Base Statistics

Broad-shouldered and athletic, slag mays demonstrate incredible talent for all physical tasks. They share a vivacity, an addiction to new sights and experiences, and a tendency to collect souvenirs wherever they go.

Ancestry Annis hag
Typical Alignment CN
Ability Modifiers +2 Str, +2 Cha, -2 Con
Hag Racial Trait Annis-born changelings gain a +1 racial bonus on melee damage rolls.
Awakened Hag Heritage Your jagged claws cause gaping wounds, dealing 1 point of bleed damage on a successful critical hit.
uvFRWmR4IU1kPbyM.name: Annis-Born (Slag May)

uydqYbFH4Y5cktKs.description:
Race: Kobold
Replaced Trait(s): Crafty

Traversing the tunnels near big, nasty, hungry creatures, you learned to keep quiet-and to keep your spells quiet too. You gain a +2 racial bonus on Stealth checks, and once per day you can gain the benefit of the Silent Spell feat on a spell you cast. This racial trait replaces crafty.
uydqYbFH4Y5cktKs.name: Spellcaster Sneak

v2usdAAHs5dROa65.description:
Race: Dhampir - Ancient-Born
Replaced Trait(s): Manipulative

Alternate Skill Modifiers: Climb, Survival
v2usdAAHs5dROa65.name: Alternate Skill Modifiers (Dhampir - Ancient-Born)

v5W731SKm6CeoMO4.description:
Race: Ghoran
Replaced Trait(s): Past-Life Knowledge; Nature Magic

Ghorans were originally created to survive and thrive in a magic-blasted wasteland, and over the centuries some ghorans have adapted greater abilities to overcome magic in general. Once per day, the ghoran can cast dispel magic as a spell-like ability, with a caster level equal to the ghoran's character level. If the ghoran's favored class is a spellcasting class, the ghoran gains a +2 racial bonus on dispel checks (Pathfinder RPG Core Rulebook 272).

This replaces past-life knowledge and nature magic.
v5W731SKm6CeoMO4.name: Spelleater

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] racial to checks to gather information

v5fEQjunFTFRvJXT.description:
Race: Gathlain
Replaced Trait(s): Spell-Like Abilities

Many gathlains excitedly share every last bit of gossip they can find. These gathlains gain a +2 racial bonus on Diplomacy checks to gather information and on checks with one Knowledge skill of the gathlain's choice. Once the choice of Knowledge skill is made, it can't be changed.

This replaces spell-like abilities.

Note

Choose Knowledge skill under the Changes tab
v5fEQjunFTFRvJXT.name: Otherworldly Gossip

v6db0gftaAJKUfvK.description:
Race: Half-Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Multitalented

Half-elves occasionally seek tutoring to help them master the magic in their blood. Half-elves with this racial trait have only one favored class and it must be an arcane spellcasting class. They can use spell trigger and spell completion items for their favored class as if 1 level higher (or as a 1st-level character if they have no levels in that class). This racial trait replaces the multitalented racial trait.

Note

Choose a single arcane spellcasting class as your sole favored class. You must manually adjust your level in conjunction with the noted item types.
v6db0gftaAJKUfvK.name: Arcane Training

v70XALZVLU2APfJ9.description:
Race: Dwarf
Replaced Trait(s): Darkvision

Many feyborn have low-light vision. Races that normally have darkvision (such as dwarves and half-orcs) can take low-light vision in place of darkvision.
v70XALZVLU2APfJ9.name: Low-Light Vision (Dwarf)

vDrJaVZaxQM6AreB.description:
Race: Gathlain
Replaced Trait(s): Natural Armor

Some gathlain seeds find their way to the Hanging Bower, the realm of the alluring Eldest known as the Green Mother. Gathlains grown in the Hanging Bower sprout beautiful but delicate hibiscus flowers on their vines and emit a seductive pheromone. Gathlains with this trait gain a +2 racial bonus on Diplomacy and Handle Animal checks. This racial trait replaces natural armor.
vDrJaVZaxQM6AreB.name: Bower Born

contextNotes.text: -[[2]] penalty against fear effects.

vGtt49PJQtdsOIC3.description:
Race: Halfling
Replaced Trait(s): Fearless

Song'o halflings rarely grow up in one place, as they are quick to move to a new home whenever they are discovered. Halflings with this racial trait gain a +2 racial bonus on Reflex saves, but they take a -2 penalty on saving throws against fear effects.

This replaces fearless.
vGtt49PJQtdsOIC3.name: Evasive Nomad

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Racial bonus to repair ships.
+[[1]] Racial bonus on damage rolls against foes and objects in, or on top of, the water.

vLOP9cyjnqOUGUGr.description:
Race: Half-Orc
Replaced Trait(s): Darkvision

In the Lands of the Linnorm Kings and the Shackles, half-orcs frequently take up sailing as pirates, raiders, and fishers, where they are known as tenacious fighters. They gain a +2 racial bonus on Profession (sailor) checks and Craft checks to repair ships, and a +1 racial bonus on damage rolls against foes and objects in or on top of the water. This racial trait replaces darkvision.

Note

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the Profession (sailor) skill.

Update the target of the Change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect Profession (sailor)
vLOP9cyjnqOUGUGr.name: Sea Raider

vPj2ndv0gWWlpa4X.description:
Race: Half-Orc
Replaced Trait(s): Orc Ferocity, Weapon Familiarity

Some half-orcs raised as humans lack their cousins' ferocity and training in orc weapons, but pick up a bit of their human parents' skills. They gain the human's skilled racial trait. This racial trait replaces orc ferocity and weapon familiarity.
vPj2ndv0gWWlpa4X.name: Human-Raised (Half-Orc)

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Racial bonus to resist bull rush or trip.

vXfdoGjzMenvozlp.description:
Race: Vanara
Replaced Trait(s): Prehensile Tail

The rare whitecape vanara clans have more in common with savanna-dwelling baboons than the jungle-dwelling monkeys that most vanaras resemble. Their stooping build grants them a +4 racial bonus to CMD when resisting a bull rush or trip. These vanaras usually have a thick mane of hair on the head and shoulders (called a "cape") and shorter tails. This racial trait replaces prehensile tail.
vXfdoGjzMenvozlp.name: Whitecape

contextNotes.text: You deal 50% weapon damage to incorporeal creatures when using nonmagical weapons (including natural and unarmed attacks), as if using magic weapons.
+[[2]] bonus on saving throws to remove negative levels,
You recover physical ability damage from attacks by undead creatures at a rate of 2 points per ability score per day (rather than the normal 1 point per ability score per day).
You deal 50% weapon damage to incorporeal creatures when using nonmagical weapons (including natural and unarmed attacks), as if using magic weapons.

vZvTS5zeW4AVNARd.description:
Race: Half-Orc
Replaced Trait(s): Weapon Familiarity

Those who understand the connection between shadows and the Negative Energy Plane know how to fight the spirits of darkness. Characters with this trait deal 50% weapon damage to incorporeal creatures when using nonmagical weapons (including natural and unarmed attacks), as if using magic weapons. They also gain a +2 bonus on saving throws to remove negative levels, and recover physical ability damage from attacks by undead creatures at a rate of 2 points per ability score per day (rather than the normal 1 point per ability score per day).

Half-orcs can take this trait in place of weapon familiarity.
vZvTS5zeW4AVNARd.name: Shadowhunter (Half-Orc)

vjGYESFbD0sfHJjq.description:
Race: Half-Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Keen Senses

The character has a mystic connection to one terrain type, selected from the ranger's favored terrain list. The character selects three 0-level druid spells and one 1st-level druid spell. If the character has a Charisma score of 11 or higher, when in the selected terrain, she gains these spells as spell-like abilities that can be cast once per day. The caster level for these effects is equal to the user's character level. The DC for the spell-like abilities is equal to 10 + the spell's level + the user's Charisma modifier. These spells are treated as being from a fey source for the purposes of the druid's resist nature's lure class feature and similar abilities.

A half-elf can take this trait in place of keen senses.

Note

Select one terrain type from the ranger's favored terrain list and select three 0-level druid spells and one 1st-level druid spell when you take this trait.

update the details tab of this trait accordingly.
vjGYESFbD0sfHJjq.name: Fey Magic (Half-Elf)

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Racial bonus to convince opponent of a lie.

vt3mK2P7RkUqA7nE.description:
Race: Tiefling
Replaced Trait(s): Skilled

Many tieflings find that the best way to get along in the world is to tell others what they want to hear. These tieflings' practice of telling habitual falsehoods grants them a +4 racial bonus on Bluff checks to convince an opponent that what they are saying is true when they tell a lie. This racial trait replaces skilled.
vt3mK2P7RkUqA7nE.name: Beguiling Liar

vyJuXppTrAQZWfE0.description:
Race: Kobold
Replaced Trait(s): Crafty

By observing the noises in underground tunnels, you learned to uncannily mimic any voice or sound you heard. Three times per day, you can attempt to trick someone in this way by making a Bluff check against the listener's Sense Motive check. You get a +2 circumstance bonus on this check if you're in tunnels or other structures where your voice can echo. This racial trait replaces crafty.
vyJuXppTrAQZWfE0.name: Echo Whistler

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Racial bonus to identify undead; you can attempt such checks untrained.

w24Veg7ttOZfc7eF.description:
Race: Dwarf
Replaced Trait(s): Stonecunning

Dwarves with this racial trait gain a +2 racial bonus on Knowledge (religion) checks to identify undead and can attempt them untrained. This racial trait replaces stonecunning.
w24Veg7ttOZfc7eF.name: Barrow Scholar

w3g6qgyPTrfa7x2x.description:
Race: Sylph
Replaced Trait(s): Air Affinity

A sylph with this racial trait gains fast healing 2 for 1 round anytime she takes electricity damage (whether or not this electricity damage gets through her electricity resistance). The sylph can heal up to 2 hit points per level per day with this ability, after which it ceases to function. This racial trait replaces air affinity.
w3g6qgyPTrfa7x2x.name: Storm in the Blood

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Racial bonus to independently research spells, create magic items they have never encountered before, and identify unique magical effects.
+[[2]] Racial bonus to independently research spells, create magic items they have never encountered before, and identify unique magical effects.
+[[2]] Racial bonus to persuade others to adopt a new ideology or further the cause of discovery and progress.

wDBLqYPQAER9KNFY.description:
Race: Human
Replaced Trait(s): Skilled

Humans have come to shape the face of Golarion because they are inveterate innovators. Humans with this racial trait gain a +2 racial bonus on Knowledge (arcana) and Spellcraft checks to independently research spells, create magic items they have never encountered before, and identify unique magical effects. They also gain a +2 racial bonus on Charisma-based skill checks to persuade others to adopt a new ideology or further the cause of discovery and progress. This racial trait replaces skilled.
wDBLqYPQAER9KNFY.name: Innovative

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Racial bonus regarding dwarves or their enemies, and make such checks without training.

wG1e906wkQGlk3eE.description:
Race: Dwarf
Replaced Trait(s): Greed

Dwarves keep extensive records about their history and the world around them. Dwarves with this racial trait receive a +2 racial bonus on Knowledge (history) checks that pertain to dwarves or their enemies. They can make such skill checks untrained. This racial trait replaces greed.
wG1e906wkQGlk3eE.name: Lorekeeper

contextNotes.text:
+[[2]] Racial bonus vs. spells and spell-like abilities of fey. (stacks with 
Illusion Resistance)
+[[2]] Racial bonus vs. fey.
+[[2]] Racial bonus vs. fey.
+[[2]] Racial bonus vs. fey.
+[[2]] bonus on caster level checks to overcome the SR of fey

wNE1rlrOUrO4IKnP.description:
Race: Gnome
Replaced Trait(s): Defensive Training, Hatred, Keen Senses

Some gnomes are especially sensitive to the presence of fey and receive a +2 racial bonus on Perception, Sense Motive, and Survival checks against fey, a +2 racial bonus on saving throws against the spells, spell-like abilities, and supernatural abilities of fey (this bonus stacks with the bonus granted by illusion resistance), and a +2 bonus on caster level checks to overcome the spell resistance of fey. The racial trait replaces defensive training, hatred, and keen senses.
wNE1rlrOUrO4IKnP.name: Fairy Catcher

wQnT357k2EwEAT49.description:
Race: Half-Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Base Statistics

Few in number and rarely seen, Spireborn retain many human features but are still as enigmatic as their elven forebears.

Elven Ancestry Mordant Spire elves
Human Ancestry Arcadian, Ulfen, Varisian
Regions Mordant Spire (Steaming Sea)
Languages Azlanti, Elven

Mordant Spire elves and humans almost never meet by accident. Their offspring, the Spireborn, only come about when Mordant Spire elves travel to human villages. Spireborn half-elves are almost always taken back to the Mordant Spire by their elven parent, and they are usually trained from youth to go on important missions outside of the Mordant Spire. Spireborn undergo both physical training and extensive education on the Azlanti. They tell no one they meet on their travels of their heritage or their top-secret duties.

Note

alternate racial traits available to spireborn

Mordant Envoy

Languages (Spireborn)
wQnT357k2EwEAT49.name: Mordant Spire Half-Elves (Spireborn)

wUzya0Ps91Bo8xDG.description:
Race: Human
Replaced Trait(s): Bonus Feat

Humans have great intuition for hidden potential. They gain a +2 bonus on Sense Motive checks. In addition, when they acquire an animal companion, bonded mount, cohort, or familiar, that creature gains a +2 bonus to one ability score of the character's choice. This racial trait replaces the bonus feat racial trait.

Note

You must update the companion creature's abilities, when acquired, manually.
wUzya0Ps91Bo8xDG.name: Eye for Talent

whBrPvSquuUlVglw.description:
Race: Human
Replaced Trait(s): Bonus Feat

Humans raised within monastic traditions or communities that encourage mindfulness seem to shrug off many dangers more easily than other humans. They gain a +1 racial bonus on all saving throws and concentration checks. This racial trait replaces humans' bonus feat.
whBrPvSquuUlVglw.name: Awareness

contextNotes.text: +[[1]]  Racial Bonus vs language-dependent effects.

wjJMLojRWBvteEod.description:
Race: Sahuagin
Replaced Trait(s): Speak with Sharks

While Outsear sahuagin typically don't have a history of music, over the years the merfolk of the city have shared their love of song with them. Sahuagin with this racial trait gain a +2 racial bonus on Perform (sing) checks and a +1 racial bonus on saves against language-dependent effects.

This replaces speak with sharks.

Note

Update the Skills tab of your Actor to add the Perform (sing) skill. Update the target of the untargeted change on the Changes tab of this trait to reflect Perform (sing).
wjJMLojRWBvteEod.name: Sahuagin Song

wjTQfaGzCVey4pZe.description:
Race: Gnome
Replaced Trait(s): Base Statistics

These gnomes were born on the move, their families living on a ship or in a caravan wagon. These gnomes have the Magical Linguist and either the explorer or the Gift of Tongues alternate racial traits.
wjTQfaGzCVey4pZe.name: Traveler Gnomes

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Racial bonus vs. illusions from non-aboleths
-2 penalty vs. illusions from aboleths

wo6yWJXwBCZjPNHm.description:
Race: Gillman
Replaced Trait(s): Enchantment Resistance

Gillmen with this racial trait gain a +2 racial bonus on saving throws against non-aboleth illusion spells or effects, but they take a -2 penalty on such saving throws against aboleth sources. This racial trait replaces enchantment resistance.
wo6yWJXwBCZjPNHm.name: Truthseer

wrqUJsULTxngAMQb.description:
Race: Skinwalker
Replaced Trait(s): Base Statistics

Ancestry wereshark
Typical Alignment N
Ability Modifiers +2 Wis, -2 Int (+2 Con while shapechanged)
Alternate Skill Modifiers Perception, Swim
Alternate Spell-Like Ability charm animal (aquatic animals only) 3/day
Bestial Features

  • Amphibious
  • Bite attack that deals 1d6 points of damage
  • Ferocity (Pathfinder RPG Bestiary 300)
  • Swim speed of @Distance[30 ft;dual]
wrqUJsULTxngAMQb.name: Wereshark-Kin (Seascarred)

wtvFYVd0yz9QSkKL.description:
Race: Drow
Replaced Trait(s): Drow Immunities, Keen Senses

The Darklands is full of strange energies, and the drow or half-drow who live there practice bloody rituals in exchange for favor and power. Drow or half-elves with this trait gain the Psychic Sensitivity feat (Pathfinder Roleplaying Game Occult Adventures 138), gaining access to occult skill unlocks normally only available to psychic spellcasters.

This racial trait replaces keen senses and drow immunities.
wtvFYVd0yz9QSkKL.name: Psychic Sensitivity

x2lTx9jhNl0sLqb8.description:
Race: Half-Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Keen Senses, Low-Light Vision

Rather than having bodily and facial features that are a blend of elven and human, or tending towards one parent or the other, a rare few half-elves have a strange combination of features. They have noticeable mismatched eyes and ears, as well as uneven limbs. Half-elves with this trait take a -2 penalty on Reflex saves but receive a +4 racial bonus on initiative checks. This racial trait replaces keen senses and low-light vision.
x2lTx9jhNl0sLqb8.name: Mismatched

x2zsfToayeBdnrvD.description:
Race: Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Weapon Familiarity

Elves with this racial trait have a base move speed of @Distance[35 ft;dual]. This racial trait replaces weapon familiarity.
x2zsfToayeBdnrvD.name: Long-Limbed

contextNotes.text: +[[1]] Racial bonus as part of readied action
+[[1]] Racial bonus as part of readied action

x3JL4aygweVL6wvo.description:
Race: Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Elven Magic, Weapon Familiarity

Elves who frequently interact with shorterlived species, especially members who hail from Varisia, often come to rely on their ability to weight out such impatient races. These elves gain a +1 racial bonus on attack rolls and skill checks made as part of readied actions in combat. This racial trait replaces the elven magic and weapon familiarity traits.
x3JL4aygweVL6wvo.name: Disinterested Observer

x3xSdE7wj2wO63K5.description:
Race: Gathlain
Replaced Trait(s): Normal Speed (Gathlain)

Occasionally the conditions for a gathlain seed's growth heavily favor the plant portion of its body, causing more nutrients to go to the building of its vine wings and fewer to developing the gathlain's legs. These gathlains have larger wings than normal. They have a base speed of @Distance[20 ft;dual] and a fly speed of @Distance[40 ft;dual] (average). This racial trait alters the gathlain's speeds.

Note

Remove Normal Speed (Gathlain) racial trait to ensure accurate land and fly speeds. You must set your maneuverability to Average on your actor manually
x3xSdE7wj2wO63K5.name: Symbiotic Imbalance

x8hE3PC9FPU2MM95.description:
Race: Gathlain
Replaced Trait(s): Low-Light Vision

Occasionally the tendrils of a gathlain's symbiotic vines wrap around the gathlain's face like a leafy mask. The vines supplement the gathlain's vision with photosynthetic receptors. Gathlains with this racial trait gain a +2 racial bonus on Perception checks. Photosynthetic vision replaces low-light vision.
x8hE3PC9FPU2MM95.name: Photosynthetic Vision

xCZNyb4ZbM34FYxZ.description:
Race: Dwarf
Replaced Trait(s): Base Statistics

The Pahmet have been present in Osirion since ancient days and claim to have been sent by the gods to warn the pharaohs of impending crises and disasters. Though most scholars suspect that claim is more legend than history, the ancient pharaohs of Osirion treated the Pahmet with great reverence. In return, the Pahmet guard Erekrus, a necropolis in the mountains of southern Osirion, where the Pahmet interred the bodies of pharaohs deemed particularly worthy. The Pahmet are a rigid and often mistrusting people; it is common for its members who avoid contact with outsiders to have the Xenophobic alternate racial trait. Many Pahmet have the Lorekeeper, Mountaineer, Rock Stepper, and Surface Survivalist alternate racial traits. Those who come from long lines of keepers who dedicate themselves to the guarding of tombs also often have the Death's End alternate racial trait described below.
xCZNyb4ZbM34FYxZ.name: Pahmet Dwarves

xN5RoU7EhodHzdld.description:
Race: Gnome
Replaced Trait(s): Gnome Magic

Gnomes with this trait gain haunted fey aspect and speak with plants instead of dancing lights and speak with animals as spell-like abilities from the gnome magic racial trait. This racial trait alters the gnome magic racial trait.

Note

Remove Gnome Magic (Gnome)
xN5RoU7EhodHzdld.name: Fey-Tongued

xPtJg4WPKxVRmVdM.description:
Race: Goblin
Replaced Trait(s): Base Statistics

A few goblins attain a much larger size than their kin. No one is exactly sure why they grow to be giants among their kind, but it's probably due to a combination of luck, diet, and constant access to food. These goblins are monsters among their own kind, not just in height, but also in girth and in strength. If not cast out for eating all of the tribe's food, oversized goblins often become the bosses of their tribes, and the most powerful of them become chiefs.

Oversized goblins are Medium size, and grow to @Distance[4 ft;dual] to @Distance[5 ft;dual] tall. They tend to be particularly obese, weighing between @Weight[225;dual] and @Weight[275;dual]. Instead of the normal racial ability score modifiers for goblins, oversized goblins gain a +2 bonus to Strength, a +2 bonus to Dexterity, and a -2 penalty to Charisma.
xPtJg4WPKxVRmVdM.name: Oversized Goblins

xZm1SIpjHpL6UTgs.description:
Race: Fetchling
Replaced Trait(s): Spell-Like Abilities

Some fetchlings learn how to deflect unwanted attention. Fetchlings with this racial trait can use Sanctuary once per day as a spell-like ability. When such a fetchling reaches 9th level in any combination of classes, he gains Nondetection (self only) as a spell-like ability usable once per day, and at 13th level, he gains Veil (self only) usable once per day. A fetchling's caster level is equal to his total Hit Dice. This racial trait modifies the fetchling's spell-like abilities racial trait.

Note

this trait replaces

Spell-Like Abilities (Fetchling - Disguise Self)

Spell-Like Abilities (Fetchling - Plane Shift)

Spell-Like Abilities (Fetchling - Shadow Walk)
xZm1SIpjHpL6UTgs.name: Nidalese Recluse

xfVICBMFK0eGdT83.description:
Race: Dwarf
Replaced Trait(s): Stability

Dwarves with this racial trait gain a +2 bonus on Diplomacy and Sense Motive checks, and Diplomacy is a class skill for such dwarves.

This replaces stability.
xfVICBMFK0eGdT83.name: Iron Citizen

xhSNgXRtupTp2rWh.description:
Race: Halfling
Replaced Trait(s): Fearless

The character sees the world more like a native of the First World. Select two of the following skills: Acrobatics, Bluff, Climb, Diplomacy, Disguise, Escape Artist, Fly, Knowledge (nature), Perception, Perform, Sense Motive, Sleight of Hand, Stealth, Swim, or Use Magic Device. The selected skills are always class skills for the character.

A halfling can take it in place of fearless.

Note

Select any two (2) of the skills noted above when you select this trait.

Update the Class skills section of the Details tab of this trait to reflect your choices.
xhSNgXRtupTp2rWh.name: Fey Thoughts (Halfling)

xn5DcmSjk5NIPJjN.description:
Race: Fetchling
Replaced Trait(s): Spell-Like Abilities

Some fetchlings gain an upsetting gaze attack that they can activate three times per day as a standard action, affecting all creatures within a @Distance[30 ft;dual] cone that can see. All creatures that meet the fetchling's unnerving gaze are shaken for 1 round (DC = 10 + 1/2 the fetchling's character level + her Charisma modifier; Will negates). When the fetchling reaches 9th level, her gaze instead causes creatures to be staggered. When the fetchling reaches 13th level, her gaze instead causes creatures to be stunned. Unnerving gaze is a mind-affecting fear effect. This racial trait replaces the fetchling's spell-like abilities.
xn5DcmSjk5NIPJjN.name: Unnerving Gaze

contextNotes.text: -4 if using tentacles instead of hands

xy56alprFFyhWR0y.description:
Race: Cecaelia
Replaced Trait(s): Ink Cloud

Occasionally a cecaelia is born with unusually long and agile tentacles. These tentacles allow the cecaelia to sense a greater area and to hold small items. A cecaelias with this racial trait increases the range of her tentacle sense to @Distance[30 ft;dual]. In addition, a cecaelia with this racial trait can attempt Sleight of Hand checks with her tentacles rather than her hands but with a -4 penalty. This racial trait replaces ink cloud.
xy56alprFFyhWR0y.name: Dexterous Tentacles

contextNotes.text: Total of +[[4]] Racial bonus against other humanoids. additional +[[2]] Racial bonus to feign ignorance.
Total of +[[4]] Racial bonus against other humanoids.
Total of +[[4]] Racial bonus against other humanoids. Additional +[[2]] racial bonus to intercept secret messages.

y5Qym74bY1fzDf5A.description:
Race: Half-Orc
Replaced Trait(s): Intimidating, Orc Ferocity, Weapon Familiarity

Some half-orcs use half-orcs' brutish stereotypes to their advantage, causing others to underestimate their intelligence and scheming. Such half-orcs gain a +2 racial bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, and Sense Motive checks. This bonus increases to +4 against other humanoids. They also receive a +2 racial bonus on Bluff checks to feign ignorance and Sense Motive checks to intercept secret messages, and this stacks with the above bonus. This racial trait replaces intimidating, orc ferocity, and weapon familiarity.
y5Qym74bY1fzDf5A.name: Overlooked Mastermind

contextNotes.text: -5 penalty to hide from creatures distracted by Bluff (instead of -10)

y7DoDbr2CQZOfmvf.description:
Race: Ratfolk
Replaced Trait(s): Tinker

Some ratfolk can blend easily into their environments, and move with surprising grace. Ratfolk gain a +2 racial bonus on Stealth checks, and take only a -5 penalty on Stealth checks made to hide from creatures they have distracted with a Bluff check (rather than the normal -10 penalty). This racial trait replaces tinker.
y7DoDbr2CQZOfmvf.name: Skulk

y7ONadfWrrdFYIw4.description:
Race: Gnome
Replaced Trait(s): Base Statistics

The city of Segada on the continent of Arcadia has a substantial gnomish population, which emigrated from the tall peaks of the Akrandida Mountains. Known for their creativity and love all forms of artistic expression, many of Segada's gnomes make their living as entertainers. Even more so than other gnomes, Segada gnomes are fond of wearing bright colors, and often wear accessories that make noise as they move, such as bells, jangling bracelets, and flute-like reed pendants. Most Segada gnomes have the Darkvision, Gift of Tongues, and Magical Linguist alternate racial traits, as well as the artistic and Sound Mimicry alternate racial traits.
y7ONadfWrrdFYIw4.name: Segada Gnomes

contextNotes.text: You have resistance 5 to [chosen type]

yDpihlrYhH16CQtN.description:
Race: Elf
Replaced Trait(s): Elven Immunities

Elves who dwell in the most extreme environments, from arctic wastelands to volcanic plains, develop natural resistance to the dangers of their homelands over the course of a few generations. Elves with this racial trait gain elemental resistance 5 to acid, cold, electricity, or fire. This choice is made at character creation, and once made it cannot be changed. This racial trait replaces elven immunities.

Note

Choose acid, cold, electricity, or fire. you must add resistance 5 of the chosen type to attributes tab of your actor manually. Update the context note on the changes tab of this trait to match your chosen type.
yDpihlrYhH16CQtN.name: Elemental Resistance

yH3vOEPKWRtwqQj9.description:
Race: Gathlain
Replaced Trait(s): Racial Fly Speed

Some gathlains draw exceptional nourishment from any kind of tree through their wings, although their wings are much more delicate. A gathlain with this racial trait has a fly speed of only @Distance[20 ft;dual] (poor maneuverability) but gains fast healing 2 for 1 round whenever he is adjacent to a living tree larger than he is. The gathlain can heal up to 2 hit points per level per day with this ability, after which it ceases to function.

This modifies the gathlain's fly speed.

Note

Remove Normal Speed (Gathlain) racial trait to ensure accurate land and fly speeds.

You must set your maneuverability to poor on your actor manually
yH3vOEPKWRtwqQj9.name: Arboreal Vitality

yQFgtsFjg71gdHoO.description:
Race: Human
Replaced Trait(s): Bonus Feat

All humans are skillful, but some, rather than being generalists, tend to specialize in a handful of skills. At 1st, 8th, and 16th level, such humans gain Skill Focus in a skill of their choice as a bonus feat. This racial trait replaces the bonus feat trait.

Note

There are multiple versions of the Skill Focus feat, one for each skill, and those specific feats must be added to your actor separately once you have selected a skill.
yQFgtsFjg71gdHoO.name: Focused Study

yRISJZyDwhGV0jFO.description:
Race: Undine
Replaced Trait(s): Base Statistics

Soothing and insightful, mistsoul undines move through the world with airy resilience and earnest action.

Typical Alignment NG
Ability Modifiers +2 Con, +2 Wis, -2 Int
Alternate Spell-Like Ability Mistsouls gain obscuring mist as a spell-like ability.
Mist Affinity This racial trait functions as and can be replaced as the base undine's water affinity, except it benefits sorcerers with the dreamspun bloodline and spellcasters with the Repose domain.

With gentle spirits and contemplative minds, mistsouls are drawn to the more mystic aspects of their element. Their ephemeral, vaporous natures can allow them to lull others into a dreamlike state, or facilitate a peaceful transition into the next life. They tend to seek out places where ideological and political struggles converge, and thus they are at home in communities like those of the common folk of Brevoy.

Physically, mistsouls have rounded features and graceful deliberation in their movement. They prefer soft, highquality garments of neutral or earth tones and simple jewelry chosen for significance rather than expense.
yRISJZyDwhGV0jFO.name: Mistsoul (Vapor Undine)

contextNotes.text: +[[2]] Racial while benefiting from full or partial concealment due to dim light or darkness.
+[[2]] Racial while benefiting from full or partial concealment due to dim light or darkness.
+[[2]] Racial while benefiting from full or partial concealment due to dim light or darkness.

yXdKpYeTHVOwCHvS.description:
Race: Gnome
Replaced Trait(s): Weapon Familiarity

Characters with this trait are at home in gloomy conditions. Whenever these characters benefit from concealment or full concealment due to darkness or dim light, they gain a +2 racial bonus on Intimidate, Perception, and Stealth checks.

Gnomes can take this trait in place of weapon familiarity.
yXdKpYeTHVOwCHvS.name: Dimdweller (Gnome)

yd1VNCUGmvc2fcXG.description:
Race: Dwarf
Replaced Trait(s): Base Statistics

Traditionalist dwarves of ancient lineage have the Ancient Enmity, Lorekeeper, and either the Magic Resistant or Stubborn racial traits.
yd1VNCUGmvc2fcXG.name: Elder Dwarf

contextNotes.text: +[[4]] Racial bonus to climb without rope
+[[4]] Racial bonus to maintain balance.

yfJ0rhcQv12UHtau.description:
Race: Orc
Replaced Trait(s): Ferocity

Mountain-dwelling orcs thrive on taking insane risks to defeat foes and show up rivals. They gain a +4 racial bonus on Climb checks without rope and on Acrobatics checks to maintain balance. This racial trait replaces ferocity.
yfJ0rhcQv12UHtau.name: Reckless Climber

yoGRjHSNTbIJE7e3.description:
Race: Vanara
Replaced Trait(s): Nimble

Vanaras are often extraordinarily agile, capable of dancing around slower-moving humans with playful grace. They gain a +2 racial bonus on Acrobatics and Escape Artist checks. This racial trait replaces nimble.

Note

Remove Nimble (Vanara)
yoGRjHSNTbIJE7e3.name: Acrobatic (Vanara)

yodj1nKWZ1hzQVVv.description:
Race: Dhampir
Replaced Trait(s): Base Statistics

With skin like faded brass, ajibachanas reveal their undead taint through their unnatural agility and childlike trains of thought.

Ancestry Vetala
